summary: D12-scoped round 6: build/typecheck/388-unit(29 files, incl. all 3 D12 guards)/79-e2e all green + hands-on (static PNG + live interactive drag) confirm the API Gateway fan de-knot holds statically and after a real drag, 0 app console errors, byte-identical determinism x2. e2e initially found 2 pre-existing (NOT D12-caused, confirmed via a disable-bends control test) stale test-file assertions from the round-7 FR7 arc-to-gap pivot never exercised by e2e before D12 (TEST-005 stale Q-glyph check, TEST-006 stale exact-crossing-count assumption); both fixed in-round (test-file-only, no product code touched), suite re-confirmed 79/79 green. Regenerated flowchart/class/state examples (clean+sketch, light) eyeballed clean, state's fail/retry labels confirmed off their lines. D12 verdict: GREEN, ready for report/UAT. One legacy item (TEST-004, the FR9 extreme-drag lane-convergence limitation) is carried forward unchanged, open, explicitly out of this round's D12 scope per the orchestrator's framing as a known/disclosed pre-D12 limitation - not re-tested this round, not resolved by this round, surfaced here for the routing decision rather than silently dropped.
